name: Fondue Bread with Sliced Apples & Chutney
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Frozen white bread dough, thawed according to package directions
recipeInstructions:
1. Adjust oven rack to the middle position. Heat oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit. Grease a 9-inch springform pan with butter or margarine.
2. Reserve 1 egg white for later use. Beat the yolks of 2 eggs and 1 egg white in a medium bowl. Add the cheese and rosemary; stir to combine and set aside.
3. Roll the thawed dough on a lightly floured work surface into a rectangle about 6 inches by 20 inches. Place the cheese mixture lengthwise on the center of the dough. Fold the dough over the filling and pinch to seal the seam. Place the roll seam-side down around the outside of the prepared springform pan to make a ring; pinch the ends to seal.
4. Brush the bread with sliced almonds.
5. Bake for 1 hour. Release the pan hinge, remove the bread immediately, and allow it to rest on a wire rack for 15 minutes before serving. Slice the apples and place them in a bowl of cold water mixed with 1 tablespoon lemon juice.
6. Place the cheese-filled bread in the center of a large round platter. Drain the sliced apples and arrange them overlapping in small groupings around the bread. Garnish with sprigs of fresh rosemary, if desired. Serve with chutney, if desired. Cut the bread into wedges.
Advance preparation: Assemble and freeze, unbaked. Thaw for 6 hours in the refrigerator. Increase baking time by 5 minutes. Note: The cheese will shred more easily in the food processor if it is very cold. Place it in the freezer for 10 minutes before grating.
